name: Enchiladas Verdes
procedure: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C).
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add onions and garlic, sauté until translucent. Add shredded chicken, season with salt and pepper, and cook for 2-3 minutes.
In a bowl, mix green enchilada sauce with sour cream until well combined.
Dip each tortilla in the enchilada sauce mixture. Fill each tortilla with the chicken mixture, roll up, and place seam-side down in a baking dish.
Pour the remaining sauce over the enchiladas and sprinkle with Monterey Jack and cheddar cheeses.
Bake for 20-25 minutes until cheese is melted and bubbly.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve hot.

name: Creamy Tuscan Chicken with Sun-Dried Tomatoes and Spinach
procedure: Season chicken breasts with salt, pepper, and dried oregano.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add chicken breasts and cook until golden brown and cooked through, about 6-8 minutes per side. Remove from skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add garlic and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add chicken broth, heavy cream, sun-dried tomatoes, and Parmesan cheese. Stir to combine and bring to a simmer.
Add spinach and cook until wilted.
Return the chicken breasts to the skillet, spooning sauce over the chicken.
Serve hot with the creamy sauce and vegetables.

name: Pad Thai
procedure: Cook rice noodles according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a small bowl, mix fish sauce, tamarind paste, soy sauce, and brown sugar. Set aside.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic and sauté until fragrant.
Add tofu and shrimp, cook until shrimp are pink and tofu is golden brown.
Push tofu and shrimp to the side of the skillet and add eggs. Scramble until fully cooked.
Add cooked noodles and sauce mixture to the skillet. Toss to combine.
Stir in bean sprouts and green onions.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped peanuts and lime wedges.

name: Roasted Vegetable and Quinoa Salad with Lemon-Herb Vinaigrette
procedure: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C).
Rinse quinoa under cold water. In a medium pot, bring quinoa and water to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until water is absorbed. Fluff with a fork and set aside.
In a large bowl, toss chopped vegetables with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking sheet and roast for 20-25 minutes until tender and slightly caramelized.
In a small bowl, whisk together lemon juice, Dijon mustard, garlic, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Slowly whisk in olive oil until well combined.
In a large bowl, combine cooked quinoa, roasted vegetables, parsley, and basil. Drizzle with lemon-herb vinaigrette and toss to combine.
Serve salad warm or chilled.

name: Ratatouille
procedure: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C).
In a large bowl, toss eggplant, zucchini, yellow squash, bell peppers, and onion with olive oil, garlic, dried thyme, salt, and pepper.
Spread vegetables in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast for 30-35 minutes until tender.
In a large skillet, heat tomato sauce over medium heat. Add roasted vegetables and stir to combine.
Simmer for 10-15 minutes until flavors meld.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh basil.

name: Moussaka
procedure: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C).
Sprinkle eggplant slices with salt and let sit for 30 minutes to draw out moisture. Rinse and pat dry.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Fry eggplant slices until golden brown on both sides. Set aside on paper towels to drain.
In the same skillet, cook ground lamb with onions and garlic until browned. Add tomato sauce, red wine, cinnamon, oregano, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 20 minutes until thickened.
In a baking dish, layer eggplant slices and meat sauce. Pour béchamel sauce over the top and sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese.
Bake for 30-35 minutes until the top is golden and bubbling.
Let cool for 10 minutes before serving.

name: Beef Wellington
procedure: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C).
Season beef tenderloin with salt and pepper. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over high heat. Sear beef on all sides until browned. Remove and let cool.
In the same skillet, add mushrooms and garlic. Cook until mushrooms release their moisture and it evaporates. Let cool.
Brush beef with Dijon mustard. Spread mushroom mixture over prosciutto slices and wrap around the beef.
Roll out puff pastry and wrap around the beef. Seal edges and brush with beaten egg.
Place beef on a baking sheet and bake for 25-30 minutes until pastry is golden brown and beef reaches desired doneness.
Let rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
